messages.content: I have a react component that is using rc-select library. When I click on select my container with the icon of arrow gets attribute unselectable='on'. What does it mean and do I have the means to change it?
can I do it some other way?
any other way?
Can I remove inselectable attribute using styled ocmponents?
In my React app how do I write a function that will console.log an element I clock on the page?

using rc-select library how do I attach handleClick event to an arrow in the select if my select is also searchable. I have a problem that it opens menu of options when I focus on the input but it closes only when I click outside of select input and ignores the arrow inside input that should close it as well.
I get the error ' Uncaught TypeError: selectRef.current.contains is not a function'

I have a react component that has some other elements within it. I don't have direct access to those elements, I can only interact with my component. Inside of it there should be an input element. How can add useRef to that input if I only have access to my container which is the parent element of it?
I need a certain input element and an icon in React. I want to create OnFocus function that will focus inside input element by clicking on the icon
Using React useRef how can I find a child component within the parent component?
What if the child component is inside Parent component and it can't be seen in the file?

I have this selector named const child =  selectRef?.current.children[0]. Within this element there are other containers and somewhere within them there is an input element. How do I modify my selector so it would target that input?
